beats.narration: Lift a surface coil a hair off the metal and you make the strongest signal it ever produces. That gap is lift-off.

payload.title: LIFT-OFF
payload.sub: The coupling variable you touch most.
payload.panelLabel: ET PROBE // GAP WRITES THE SIGNAL

beats.narration: Lift-off is the impedance change when probe spacing varies. Coupling falls almost exponentially, so the first tenth-millimetre moves it most.

payload.heading: Why the gap is so loud

beats.narration: Every lift-off locus leaves its operating point and heads for the air point. Going down the curve opens the angle to a crack.

payload.heading: The lift-off locus heads for air
payload.note: OPERATING POINT → AIR POINT · Δφ TO CRACK OPENS DOWN THE CURVE

beats.narration: To kill it as noise: null on the part, lift onto a thin shim, rotate that excursion flat, then alarm only on vertical.

payload.heading: Suppress it: rotate flat, gate the Y

chips.s: Crack breaks up, into the gate

beats.narration: One locus, two jobs. Suppress it for cracks; calibrate it on bare metal and the gap reads coating thickness. Prove the notch through maximum coating.

payload.sub: Coating thickness per ASTM E376, on certified shims, bare base metal.
